steps.title: Read population values from the graph at x = 0 and x = 1
steps.content: At $x = 0$ (year 2003), the curve passes through $y = 6$ thousand. At $x = 1$ (year 2004), the curve passes through $y = 9$ thousand.

steps.title: Calculate the ratio of population at x = 1 to population at x = 0
steps.content:
The ratio is:
$$\frac{y(1)}{y(0)} = \frac{9}{6} = 1.5$$
The population at $x=1$ is $1.5$ times the population at $x=0$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
